similarity_name: UMAP chemical-space cluster membership or distance
definition: Embed molecular fingerprints with UMAP, cluster the reduced features, and treat shared cluster membership or distance between UMAP coordinates as a train-eval chemical-space overlap axis. Same-cluster train examples are high-overlap; held-out or far clusters are more novel.
example_usage: python tabular_group_similarity.py --train train.csv --eval eval.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --train-id-col train_id --eval-id-col sample_id --group-col cluster_id

citations.title: UMAP-based clustering split for rigorous evaluation of AI models for virtual screening on cancer cell lines

evidence.summary: The paper describes UMAP clustering splits as a way to increase train-test molecular dissimilarity.
The paper constructs seven UMAP clusters from reduced molecular fingerprint features and holds out clusters for evaluation.
The paper validates that UMAP splits have lower maximum train-neighbor Tanimoto similarity and occupy more distinct chemical-space regions.

limitations: UMAP coordinates are stochastic unless seeds and hyperparameters are fixed.
Cluster membership is coarse; SPECTRA curves should also report continuous nearest-train fingerprint similarity when possible.
UMAP may preserve local/global structure differently across datasets.
quality_gates: Persist UMAP parameters, random seed, and cluster count.
Validate UMAP cluster novelty with an independent nearest-train similarity distribution.
Avoid treating cluster labels as ordered distances unless an explicit cluster-distance metric is defined.
